description:
You are the sysadmin on duty.  
  A project directory already exists at  
  /home/user/projects/disk_demo  

  Your goal is to audit that directory’s space consumption and
  create a concise machine-readable log.

  1. Calculate:  
     a) the total number of bytes occupied by every regular file that
        is anywhere inside /home/user/projects/disk_demo  
     b) the absolute path of the single largest regular file inside
        the same tree (if several files share the same maximum size,
        choose the one whose full path comes first in normal
        lexicographic order)  
     c) the count of **immediate** children (regular files +
        directories) that are located directly inside
        /home/user/projects/disk_demo (do not descend; “ls -1” level).  

  2. Write the results to a new text file called  
     /home/user/disk_usage_report.log  

     The file must contain exactly three lines, **in this exact order**,
     each terminated by a single line-feed character (`\n`):

       TOTAL_BYTES=<integer‐value>
       LARGEST_ITEM=<absolute-path>
       ITEM_COUNT=<integer‐value>

     • Keys are uppercase and fixed as shown.  
     • There must be no extra spaces before or after the “=” sign, no
       extra blank lines, and no trailing spaces at line ends.  
     • The file must end with a final newline.  

  3. To keep things simple, you should be able to gather all the data
     with only three or four standard GNU/Linux commands (typical
     solutions use some mix of “du -b”, “find”, “sort”, “wc -l”, etc.).
     Feel free to store intermediate results in temporary files or
     shell variables, but they must not remain after the task is done.

  4. When finished, verify the contents of
     /home/user/disk_usage_report.log by printing it to the terminal so
     the supervising engineer can immediately see the outcome.

  Notes:
  • You do NOT need elevated privileges.  
  • Do not delete or modify anything under /home/user/projects/disk_demo.  
  • Make sure the log file is world-readable (mode 644) so that the
    automated audit system can pick it up later.  
  • Only the exact format described above will be accepted by the
    automated checker.
truth:
Pre-existing structure (created before the agent starts):

    /home/user/projects/disk_demo
    ├── file1.txt          – exactly 1024 bytes
    ├── file2.log          – exactly 2048 bytes
    └── subdir
        └── data.bin       – exactly 3072 bytes

  No other files, sockets, symlinks, etc., exist anywhere under that
  directory.

  Correct results the checker expects:

    TOTAL_BYTES=6144
    LARGEST_ITEM=/home/user/projects/disk_demo/subdir/data.bin
    ITEM_COUNT=3

  Therefore the file /home/user/disk_usage_report.log must contain:

    TOTAL_BYTES=6144\n
    LARGEST_ITEM=/home/user/projects/disk_demo/subdir/data.bin\n
    ITEM_COUNT=3\n

  (Exactly three trailing newline characters, one per line; ASCII
  LF = 0x0A.)

  Permissions the test suite will verify:
    -rw-r--r-- 1 user user … /home/user/disk_usage_report.log
name: task_000000_0761a666
